source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage/Crane Incident

event.top_event: Severe injury from falling/shifting construction tool house during crane lift
actions_taken: BSEE Lafayette District conducted onsite investigation on June 7, 2017
Injured employee underwent surgery for fractured pelvis
event.summary: On May 31, 2017 at 1410 hours, an employee was severely injured when a 10,000 lb construction tool house being lifted by a platform crane came into contact with channel iron protruding from a structural member. The load shifted and struck the rigger who was positioned between the tool house and handrails, causing a fractured pelvis. Contributing factors included lack of line-of-sight communication between crane operator and rigger, ineffective radio communication, and the rigger being positioned in a pinch point hazard zone.
recommendations: BSEE Lafayette District office made no recommendations to the Regional Office of Incident Investigations
key_phrases: crane operator could not see the rigger
radios were used between the rigger and the crane operator
tool house came in contact with a piece of channel iron
third party employee attempted to get the crane operators attention to stop the lift but was unsuccessful
rigger inadvertently placed himself between the tool house and a handrail

hazards.name: Suspended load (10,000 lb construction tool house)
Obstructed structural elements (channel iron protruding from structural member)
Pinch point between load and fixed structure (handrails)

threats.name: Loss of line-of-sight communication between crane operator and rigger
Ineffective radio communication during lift operations
Load contact with obstructing structural element (channel iron)
Rigger positioned in hazardous location (pinch point zone)
Failure to detect and communicate load obstruction during lift

controls.name: Designated signal person for crane operations
controls.barrier_role: Communication and coordination between crane operator and load handler
controls.lod_basis: Wood Group GOM-SWP-Cranes and Lifting Equipment Standard 4.10; Island Operating Safe Practices Manual 28.45

supporting_text: The crane operator could not see the rigger on the cellar deck; therefore, radios were used between the rigger and the crane operator to communicate
A signal person must be provided for the following situations: 1. The point of operation is not in full view of the operator

controls.name: Radio communication system for crane operations
controls.barrier_role: Enable communication when line-of-sight is not available
controls.lod_basis: Island Operating Safe Practices Manual 28.48; Wood Group GOM-SWP-Cranes and Lifting Equipment Standard 4.10

supporting_text: the crane operator never heard the communication trying to be established with him to stop the lift
Where visual or audible signals are inadequate, telephone or portable radio communication should be used

controls.name: Pre-lift hazard identification and Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify and control hazards before lift operations commence
controls.lod_basis: Island Operating Job Safety Analysis dated May 31, 2017

supporting_text: Island Operating's Job Safety Analysis dated May 31, 2017, Section 5-Always watch out for your co-worker to insure that they are in a safe place and out of the line of fire before making lift from the platform
Number 1 task step listed on the potential hazard section of the Job Safety Analysis (JSA) focuses on poor communication not understanding hand signals

controls.name: Rigger positioning and awareness of pinch point hazards
controls.barrier_role: Prevent personnel from being positioned in hazardous locations during load movement
controls.lod_basis: Island Operating Job Safety Analysis Section 5; Industry standard safe work practices

supporting_text: The rigger inadvertently placed himself between the tool house and a handrail allowing a possible pinch point hazard
Due to the height of the tool house, the rigger could not detect any hazards associated with the lift

controls.name: Third-party observer monitoring and communication
controls.barrier_role: Detect hazards and communicate with crane operator to prevent load contact
controls.lod_basis: Observed during incident; industry best practice for load monitoring

supporting_text: During the lift, additional third party personnel were located on the drill deck looking down at the rigging operations

A third party employee observed the tool house coming in contact with the channel iron. Due to the height of the tool house, the rigger could not detect any hazards associated with the lift. The third party employee attempted to get the crane operators attention to stop the lift but was unsuccessful

controls.name: Load weight monitoring and anomaly detection
controls.barrier_role: Detect load contact with obstructions via weight indicator changes

supporting_text: The crane operator stated he noticed the weight indicator increase briefly, and then went back to the previous reading

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
